title: Darvin IDE
description: A beginner-friendly desktop IDE that lets you write and run C, C++, and SDL3 programs instantly—no compiler setup required.
achievements: In-built C/C++ compiler integration for instant setup
Native SDL3 graphics support for high-performance apps
Modern Qt-based UI with built-in terminal and editor
Portable and Installer versions for Windows students
Turbo C-like simplicity with modern C++ power
details: Darvin is designed to bring back the 'just works' experience for students. It eliminates the friction of setting up include paths, library linking, and environment variables, allowing students to focus on coding from minute one.

title: SwidoMark
description: A lightweight live Markdown editor built with SvelteKit and Tauri — featuring instant preview, clipboard support, and local storage persistence.
achievements: Live Markdown editing with real-time HTML preview
Clipboard integration for seamless copy-paste workflows
Cross-platform desktop app via Tauri — lighter and faster than Electron
Distraction-free editor interface with clean, minimal design
Persistent local storage so editing sessions survive restarts
Light, dark, and semi-transparent UI themes
details: SwidoMark pairs SvelteKit's reactive speed with Tauri's lightweight native shell. The result is a Markdown editor that launches instantly, previews in real time, and uses a fraction of the memory an Electron app would. Built for developers and writers who value focus over features.

title: Turai Blog
description: A lightweight, local blog writing app built with Tauri and React — replacing the old terminal-and-VS-Code workflow with an intuitive, interactive editor.
achievements: Interactive post creation and editing UI
Designed for a streamlined, enjoyable writing experience
details: Turai Blog replaces the friction of creating blog posts through the terminal with a clean, purpose-built editor. Manage posts, preview content, and attach cover images — all in a fast, local environment powered by Tauri and React.
